_comment: Shared, version-controlled git-tool-guard policy. Hard-deny push to main/master always; warn on destructive operations only when running under a Claude ancestor (claude_only:true). defaultMode='allow' — git is mostly safe, only specific dangerous patterns matter. See scripts/tool-guard/git/POLICY.md for design notes.

deny.message: Push to main is blocked. main = PROD (project convention: 'Never commit directly to main'). Use a feature branch → PR → merge to develop. Force push would also rewrite production history — covered by this rule.
Push to main is blocked regardless of trailing flags. main = PROD. Use a feature branch → PR → merge to develop.
Push to main is blocked. main = PROD (project convention: 'Never commit directly to main'). Use a feature branch → PR → merge to develop.
Push to main is blocked regardless of trailing flags. main = PROD.
Push to master is blocked — master is the historical PROD branch. Use a feature branch → PR.
Push to master is blocked regardless of trailing flags.
Push to master is blocked — master is the historical PROD branch. Use a feature branch → PR.
Push to master is blocked regardless of trailing flags.

warn.message: Force push under Claude detected. Force push can overwrite other people's commits on the remote. Make sure no-one else has pushed to this branch since you last pulled. Prefer --force-with-lease over --force.
Force-with-lease under Claude detected. Safer than --force, but still rewrites history on the remote. Confirm the lease ref is current.
--no-verify on push bypasses pre-push hooks (CI checks, linting). Fix the underlying issue rather than skipping the hook.
--no-verify on commit bypasses pre-commit hooks (linting, formatting). Fix the underlying issue rather than skipping the hook (project convention: 'Never skip hooks unless explicitly requested').
-n is shorthand for --no-verify. Same warning applies — bypassing hooks hides errors.
git reset --hard discards uncommitted changes permanently. Commit or stash first if you have anything you might want back.
git checkout -- <files> discards uncommitted changes in those files permanently. Commit or stash first.

Blind --theirs accepts the 'incoming' side of every conflict — context-dependent (during rebase 'theirs' = upstream; during merge 'theirs' = the merged branch). Resolve conflicts manually rather than blanket-accepting one side.
merge -X theirs blindly accepts the merged branch's side at every conflict. Can silently overwrite your work. Resolve conflicts manually.
Same as -X theirs — blindly accepts incoming. Resolve conflicts manually.
Interactive rebase requires editor input — Claude cannot interact with the rebase TODO file. Use a non-interactive rebase or do this manually.
